title: Pride and Prejudice
author: Jane Austen
description: A classic novel of family, manners, and the changing relationship between Elizabeth Bennet and Mr. Darcy.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A widely read software book focused on writing maintainable, readable, and professional code.

title: The Alchemist
author: Paulo Coelho
description: A philosophical story about a shepherd's journey to pursue purpose, meaning, and personal destiny.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt and David Thomas
description: A practical guide to software craftsmanship, problem solving, and long-term technical growth.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: An accessible exploration of cosmology, black holes, time, and the structure of the universe.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A powerful story of justice, conscience, and growing up in the racially divided American South.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
author: Martin Kleppmann
description: A modern systems book about data architecture, distributed systems, reliability, and scalability.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: A science classic that connects astronomy, evolution, history, and human curiosity in one broad narrative.

title: The Kite Runner
author: Khaled Hosseini
description: A dramatic novel about friendship, guilt, exile, and redemption across decades of Afghan history.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A landmark popular science book explaining evolution through the lens of genes and natural selection.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
author: Thomas H. Cormen, Charles E. Leiserson, Ronald L. Rivest, and Clifford Stein
description: A foundational textbook covering algorithm design, analysis, and essential computer science techniques.

title: Jane Eyre
author: Charlotte Bronte
description: A literary classic following Jane's moral and emotional journey toward independence and belonging.
